                              Brian Conor Murphy
                                              Chicago, IL 60622

Experienced software engineer with a keen interest in technology, financial markets, and continuous
learning. Over a decade of expertise in systematic trading where I have developed algorithmic
strategies for traditional futures markets, cryptocurrency markets and decentralized finance
platforms. My work has spanned all phases of the trading stack from core infrastructure to execution
logic, market microstructure/signal research to strategy ideation and strategy operation. Effective
communicator who is able to simplify complex technical concepts for various audiences while
highlighting their business rationale. Accustomed to dynamic environments, that demand crafting a
forward-looking strategic vision while remaining adaptable to change.



                                 Programming - Professional
   Python, Pydantic and computing packages:                      C++11 through C++20 for developing
   Pandas, Cython, SciKit-Learn                                  algorithmic trading strategies
   Decentralized application (Dapp) design using                 Signal research, time-series analysis and
   Web3.js and Go along with Solidity for Smart                  machine learning
   Contract development


                                             Work history
Part Time Consultant & Quant Developer
Kaleidoscope Capital, Boston, MA (Remote)                                             July 2025 - December 2025
   Engaged to design and implement a low-latency systematic futures trading platform for a newly launched
   strategy vertical.
   Architected end-to-end trading stack spanning market data ingestion, signal computation, order
   management, risk controls and execution infrastructure.
   Implemented core strategy and execution logic in modern C++ (C++17/20), emphasizing deterministic
   performance, cache-aware memory design and modular research extensibility.
   Designed CI/CD pipeline and automated testing harness to support rapid research iteration and controlled
   production deployment.
   Led evaluation and technical integration of third-party market data and exchange gateway vendors,
   including API benchmarking and reliability testing.
   Partnered directly with portfolio manager to formalize discretionary concepts into systematic signal and
   execution models.

Head of Quantitative Engineering
Striker Labs, Remote                                                                 December 2023 - May 2025
   Worked within a small team of engineers, researchers and traders to build a complete trading platform
   product for large financial institutions to access the Decentralized Finance (DeFi) space
   Abiding the needs of our initial client and backer, the platform was built to access a breadth of DeFi venues
   (across many block-chains); provide out-of-the-box yield-bearing strategies ; and provide features for all
   requisite parts of managing a systematic trading operation (i.e. data and position feeds, strategy, on-chain
   execution, risk alerting & management, and the entire front-end experience)
   Designed and implemented high-level frameworks (primarily in Python) within nascent trading platform
   (built upon Rust-based data feeds and order-management system) to enable development, research,
   deployment and operation of strategies
   Owned end-to-end risk for subset of those strategies focused on providing liquidity on UniswapV3
   marketplace across many chains
   Wrote strategy logic's heuristics and frameworks to enable experimentation with many permutations of
   strategy signals and execution models
   Researched, back-tested and identified profitable realizations of aforementioned permutations
   Built framework to reconcile back testing results against live-trading results within reason
   Built re-balancing engine that solved for sequence of operations to get strategy into its target contract
   balances
   Built majority of back-testing framework for all strategies' signal research and oversaw engineering of
   relevant, input blockchain data across phases of persistence, ingest and availability (e.g. data health metrics)

Quantitative Developer
Tower Research Capital LLC, New York, NY (Hybrid)                                      April 2023 - December 2023
   Built core risk, execution and visualization tools for upstart team deploying relative value strategies in fixed
   income and energy products across CME, ICE and EUREX
   Lead research projects around short-term execution in fixed income markets and extracting edge via market
   microstructure and order flow

Smart Contract and Backend Engineer
Steel Perlot, Remote                                                                   March 2022 - January 2023
   Lead backend engineering for vertical focused on building a human capital marketplace using a combination
   of Solidity and Go
   Lead research and development projects within special projects group to provide infrastructure to other
   verticals and develop products
   Spearheaded effort to build and run validator nodes on both Ethereum and within Cosmos networks
   Built backend for Cosmos-based inter-chain decentralized finance (DeFi) product using Go
   Built Python-based API for internal trading team to enable position entry and management across certain
   smart-contract-based DeFi protocols

Algorithmic Trader - US Treasuries
Trans Market Group LLC, Chicago, IL                                                        August 2019 - July 2021
   Designed and implemented systematic, relative-value strategy and execution logic for off-the-run treasury
   markets on nascent electronic exchanges
   Leading post-trade analysis and signal research to improve our short-term execution in treasury futures and
   cash markets.

Algorithmic Trader - Precious & Industrious Metals
Trans Market Group LLC, Chicago, IL                                                    January 2019 - August 2019
   Lead restoration of algorithmic trading presence on a desk with latency-sensitive, inter-continental arbs
   Built suite of automated performance analysis tools to assess trading software execution and networking
   infrastructure
   Conducted signal research to find novel means of improving performance and producing strategies to
   better-handle large market events.

Algorithmic Trader - Natural Gas Swaps Desk
Trans Market Group LLC, Chicago, IL                                                 November 2015 - January 2019
   Transitioned to a nascent desk where most strategies were manually executed by a single trader. Formalized
   strategies to develop algorithmic execution tools to capture market opportunities. Scaled algorithmic
   presence yielded significant P&L improvements (doubled profitability in 2016 and in 2017) along with
   increased market share. Considered one of firm's top-performing desks in 2018
   As we improved performance the desk increased headcount and I oversaw a small team tasked with research
   of short-term execution signals and microstructure inefficiencies. Additionally, oversaw development of
   strategy software.
   Worked with a team of algorithmic traders from different commodity desks to develop a generic execution
   system that could service relative value and market making strategies for all commodity desks. Developed this
   in parallel with a short-term signals module for quoting and hedging inputs.
   Utilized this execution system to develop desk's first signal-based, algorithmic market making strategy.
   Worked with head trader to discern market dynamics and order flow characteristics. End product greatly
   increased volume to enable access to a more favorable tier of trading costs.

Trade Logic Developer - US Treasuries
Trans Market Group LLC, Chicago, IL                                                 April 2013 - November 2015
   Built algorithmic risk and execution framework for desk strategies across varying degrees of discretionary
   overlay
   Managed quoting, hedging and data feed (i.e. arbitrating microwave and fiber feed) complexities between
   geographically disparate venues for Treasury cash (BrokerTech and eSpeed) and Treasury futures (CME)
   contracts
   Researched and applied new hedging signals via Bayesian classification techniques

Winter Intern for Systematic Equities Trading Group
Tudor Investment Corporation, Greenwich, CT                                         October 2012 - January 2013
   Developed Java-based, mid-office applications which provided daily P&L attribution and BARRA risk
   factor exposures for group's ETF portfolio

                                                 Projects
SigningDay.xyz November 2021 to April 2022
   Via contacts at Berkley Haas, connected with a small team to pursue this side-project to build a NFT
   marketplace (on the Ethereum blockchain) to provide college athletes a platform to connect with their
   supporters and monetize their name-image-likeness which was a monetization avenue that had just been
   approved by the NCAA
   At inception, helped devise go-to-market strategy, transaction economics of NFT marketplace and
   product/front-end design
   Learned Solidity and decentralized app development from scratch to implement marketplace's smart
   contract as well as web3-enabled front-end
   Launched product for the 2022 NCAA Men's and Women's basketball tournament
   Initial launch garnered more than $10,000 in NFT sales (based on ETH price at that time)
   etherscan link to SigningDay's smart contract
                        Additional Education & Certifications
- AWS - Certified Cloud Practitioner
- DappCamp.xyz - January 2022 to February 2022
   An immersive boot camp for onboarding to the Ethereum blockchain ecosystem and, more specifically,
   writing Smart Contracts in Solidity and broader, Dapp development
   Culminated with an independent final project for which I built a Dapp that evaluates the quality of
   underlying media storage for non-fungible tokens (NFTs)
